name: firebolt-sdk

metadata.name: firebolt-sdk
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# firebolt-sdk
[![Nightly code check](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/nightly.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/nightly.yml)
[![Unit tests](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/unit-tests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/unit-tests.yml)
[![Code quality checks](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/code-check.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/code-check.yml)
[![Firebolt Security Scan](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/security-scan.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/security-scan.yml)
[![Integration tests](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/integration-tests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-python-sdk/actions/workflows/integration-tests.yml)
![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/ptiurin/65d5a42849fd78f4c6e62fad18490d20/raw/firebolt-sdk-coverage.json)


### Installation

* Requires Python `>=3.8`
* `pip install "firebolt-sdk>=1.0.0a1"`

## Documentation

For reference and tutorials, see the [Firebolt Python SDK reference](https://python-sdk.docs.firebolt.io/).

## Connection parameters
These parameters are used to connect to a Firebolt database:
- **account_name** - name of firebolt account
- **client_id** - credentials client id
- **cliend_secret** - credentials client secret
- **database [Optional]** - name of the database to connect to
- **engine_name [Optional]** - name of the engine to connect to

## Examples
See [PEP-249](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0249) for the DB API reference and specifications. An example [jupyter notebook](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-sdk/tree/main/examples/dbapi.ipynb) is included to illustrate the use of the Firebolt API.

## Special considerations
### Cursor objects should not be shared between threads
Cursor is not thread-safe and should not be shared across threads. In a multi-threaded environment you can share a Connection, but each thread would need to keep its own Cursor. This corresponds to a thread safety 2 in the [DBApi specification](https://peps.python.org/pep-0249/#threadsafety).

### Some keywords are not allowed as SET parameters

```Python
cursor.execute("SET parameter=value")
```
This will error out if you try to set `account_id`, `output_format`, `database`, `engine`. These are special keywords that should not be set directly. To switch between databases and engines use `USE DATABASE/ENGINE`.

## Optional features
### Faster datetime with ciso8601
By default, firebolt-sdk uses `datetime` module to parse date and datetime values, which might be slow for a large amount of operations. In order to speed up datetime operations, it's possible to use [ciso8601](https://pypi.org/project/ciso8601/) package. In order to install firebolt-sdk with `ciso8601` support, run `pip install "firebolt-sdk[ciso8601]"`

## Contributing

See: [CONTRIBUTING.MD](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-sdk/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.MD)

## License
The Firebolt DB API is licensed under the [Apache License Version 2.0](https://github.com/firebolt-db/firebolt-sdk/tree/main/LICENSE) software license.

classes.name: firebolt.common.settings.Settings
documentation:
Settings for Firebolt SDK.

Attributes:
    user (Optional[str]): User name
    password (Optional[str]): User password
    access_token (Optional[str]): Access token to use for authentication
        Mutually exclusive with user and password
    account_name (Optional[str]): Account name
        Default user account is used if none provided
    server (Optional[str]): Environment api endpoint (Advanced)
        Default api endpoint is used if none provided
    default_region (str): Default region for provisioning

methods.name: firebolt.async_db.cursor.Cursor.execute
documentation:
Prepare and execute a database query.

Supported features:
    Parameterized queries: placeholder characters ('?') are substituted
        with values provided in `parameters`. Values are formatted to
        be properly recognized by database and to exclude SQL injection.
    Multi-statement queries: multiple statements, provided in a single query
        and separated by semicolon, are executed separatelly and sequentially.
        To switch to next statement result, `nextset` method should be used.
    SET statements: to provide additional query execution parameters, execute
        `SET param=value` statement before it. All parameters are stored in
        cursor object until it's closed. They can also be removed with
        `flush_parameters` method call.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query to execute
    parameters (Optional[Sequence[ParameterType]]): A sequence of substitution
        parameters. Used to replace '?' placeholders inside a query with
        actual values
    skip_parsing (bool): Flag to disable query parsing. This will
        disable parameterized, multi-statement and SET queries,
        while improving performance

Returns:
    int: Query row count.

methods.name: firebolt.async_db.cursor.Cursor.executemany
documentation:
Prepare and execute a database query.

Supports providing multiple substitution parameter sets, executing them
as multiple statements sequentially.

Supported features:
    Parameterized queries: Placeholder characters ('?') are substituted
        with values provided in `parameters`. Values are formatted to
        be properly recognized by database and to exclude SQL injection.
    Multi-statement queries: Multiple statements, provided in a single query
        and separated by semicolon, are executed separately and sequentially.
        To switch to next statement result, use `nextset` method.
    SET statements: To provide additional query execution parameters, execute
        `SET param=value` statement before it. All parameters are stored in
        cursor object until it's closed. They can also be removed with
        `flush_parameters` method call.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query to execute.
    parameters_seq (Sequence[Sequence[ParameterType]]): A sequence of
       substitution parameter sets. Used to replace '?' placeholders inside a
       query with actual values from each set in a sequence. Resulting queries
       for each subset are executed sequentially.

Returns:
    int: Query row count.

Class, responsible for executing queries to Firebolt Database.
Should not be created directly,
use {py:func}`connection.cursor <firebolt.async_db.connection.Connection>`

Args:

: description: Information about a single result row
  rowcount: The number of rows produced by last query
  closed: True if connection is closed, False otherwise
  arraysize: Read/Write, specifies the number of rows to fetch at a time

  > with the {py:func}`fetchmany` method

methods.name: firebolt.async_db.cursor.CursorV2._api_request
documentation:
Query API, return Response object.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query
    parameters (Optional[Sequence[ParameterType]]): A sequence of substitution
        parameters. Used to replace '?' placeholders inside a query with
        actual values. Note: In order to "output_format" dict value, it
            must be an empty string. If no value not specified,
            JSON_OUTPUT_FORMAT will be used.
    path (str): endpoint suffix, for example "cancel" or "status"
    use_set_parameters: Optional[bool]: Some queries will fail if additional
        set parameters are sent. Setting this to False will allow
        self._set_parameters to be ignored.

functions.name: firebolt.async_db.connection.connect_v2
documentation:
Connect to Firebolt.

Args:
    `auth` (Auth) Authentication object
    `database` (str): Name of the database to connect
    `engine_name` (Optional[str]): Name of the engine to connect to
    `account_name` (Optional[str]): For customers with multiple accounts;
                                    if none, default is used
    `api_endpoint` (str): Firebolt API endpoint. Used for authentication
    `additional_parameters` (Optional[Dict]): Dictionary of less widely-used
                            arguments for connection

classes.name: firebolt.async_db.connection.Connection

methods.name: firebolt.async_db.connection.Connection.__aenter__
documentation: Context manager support

methods.name: firebolt.async_db.connection.Connection.aclose
documentation: Close connection and all underlying cursors.

Firebolt asynchronous database connection class. Implements [PEP 249].

Args:

: `engine_url`: Firebolt database engine REST API url
  `database`: Firebolt database name
  `username`: Firebolt account username
  `password`: Firebolt account password
  `api_endpoint`: Optional. Firebolt API endpoint used for authentication
  `connector_versions`: Optional. Tuple of connector name and version, or

  > a list of tuples of your connector stack. Useful for tracking custom
  > connector usage.

Note:

: Firebolt does not support transactions,
  so commit and rollback methods are not implemented.

[pep 249]: https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0249/

Service Account authentication class for Firebolt Database.

Gets authentication token using
provided credentials and updates it when it expires.

Args:
    client_id (str): Client ID
    client_secret (str): Client secret
    use_token_cache (bool): True if token should be cached in filesystem;
        False otherwise

Attributes:
    client_id (str): Client ID
    client_secret (str): Client secret

methods.name: firebolt.client.auth.base.Auth.async_auth_flow
documentation:
Execute the authentication flow asynchronously.

Overridden in order to lock and ensure no more than
one authentication request is sent at a time. This
avoids excessive load on the auth server.

Base authentication class for Firebolt database.

Updates all http requests with bearer token authorization header

Args:
    use_token_cache (bool): True if token should be cached in filesystem;
        False otherwise

classes.name: firebolt.client.http_backend.AsyncOverriddenHttpBackend
documentation:
`OverriddenHttpBackend` is a short-term solution for the TCP
connection idle timeout issue described in the following article:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/network/network-load-balancers.html#connection-idle-timeout
Since httpx creates a connection right before executing a request, the
backend must be overridden to set the socket to `KEEPALIVE`
and `KEEPIDLE` settings.

classes.name: firebolt.client.http_backend.OverriddenHttpBackend
documentation:
`OverriddenHttpBackend` is a short-term solution for the TCP
connection idle timeout issue described in the following article:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/network/network-load-balancers.html#connection-idle-timeout
Since httpx creates a connection right before executing a request, the
backend must be overridden to set the socket to `KEEPALIVE`
and `KEEPIDLE` settings.

methods.name: firebolt.client.client.ClientV2.account_id
documentation:
User account ID.

If account_name was provided during Client construction, returns its ID;
gets default account otherwise.

Returns:
    str: Account ID

Raises:
    AccountNotFoundError: No account found with provided name

An HTTP client, based on httpx.Client.

Handles the authentication for Firebolt database.
Authentication can be passed through auth keyword as a tuple or as a
FireboltAuth instance

methods.name: firebolt.service.V2.engine.EngineService.create
documentation:
Create a new engine.

Args:
    name: An identifier that specifies the name of the engine
    region: The AWS region in which the engine runs
    engine_type: The engine type. GENERAL_PURPOSE or DATA_ANALYTICS
    spec: Firebolt instance type. If not set, will default to
        the cheapest instance.
    scale: The number of compute instances on the engine.
        The scale can be any int from 1 to 128.
    auto_stop: The amount of time (in minutes)
    after which the engine automatically stops
    warmup: The warmup method that should be used:

        `MINIMAL` - On-demand loading (both indexes and tables' data)

        `PRELOAD_INDEXES` - Load indexes only

        `PRELOAD_ALL_DATA` - Full data auto-load
        (both indexes and table data - full warmup)
    fail_if_exists: Fail is an engine with provided name already exists

Returns:
    Engine with the specified settings

methods.name: firebolt.service.V1.binding.BindingService.get_by_key
documentation: Get a binding by its BindingKey

methods.name: firebolt.service.V1.binding.BindingService.get_many
documentation:
List bindings on Firebolt, optionally filtering by database and engine.

Args:
    database_id:
        Return bindings matching the database_id.
        If None, match any databases.
    engine_id:
        Return bindings matching the engine_id.
        If None, match any engines.
    is_system_database:
        If True, return only system databases.
        If False, return only non-system databases.
        If None, do not filter on this parameter.

Returns:
    List of bindings matching the filter parameters

methods.name: firebolt.service.V1.binding.BindingService.get_database_bound_to_engine

methods.name: firebolt.service.V1.binding.BindingService.create
documentation:
Create a new binding between an engine and a database.

Args:
    engine: Engine to bind.
    database: Database to bind.
    is_default_engine:
        Whether this engine should be used as default for this database.
        Only one engine can be set as default for a single database.
        This will overwrite any existing default.

Returns:
    New binding between the engine and database.

classes.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED
documentation:
Logical record is created, however, underlying infrastructure
is not initialized.
In other words, this means that engine is stopped.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_CREATED
documentation: Engine status was created.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_PROVISIONING_PENDING
documentation: Engine initialization request was sent.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_PROVISIONING_STARTED
documentation:
Engine initialization request was received
and initialization process started.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_PROVISIONING_FINISHED
documentation: Engine initialization was finished successfully.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_PROVISIONING_FAILED
documentation: Engine initialization failed due to error.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_IDLE
documentation:
Engine is initialized,
but there are no running or starting engine revisions.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_STARTING
documentation:
Engine is initialized,
there are no running engine revisions, but it's starting.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_STARTUP_FAILED
documentation:
Engine is initialized;
initial revision failed to provision or start.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_SERVING
documentation: Engine is ready (serves an engine revision).

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_CHANGING
documentation:
Engine is ready (serves an engine revision);
zero-downtime replacement revision is starting.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_CHANGE_FAILED
documentation:
Engine is ready (serves an engine revision);
replacement revision failed to provision or start.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_RESTARTING
documentation:
Engine is initialized;
replacement of the revision with a downtime is in progress.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISION_RESTART_FAILED
documentation:
Engine is initialized;
replacement revision failed to provision or start.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_RUNNING_REVISIONS_TERMINATING
documentation:
Engine is initialized;
all child revisions are being terminated.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_TERMINATION_PENDING
documentation: Engine termination request was sent.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_TERMINATION_ST
documentation: Engine termination started.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_TERMINATION_FIN
documentation: Engine termination finished.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_TERMINATION_F
documentation: Engine termination failed.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatus.ENGINE_STATUS_DELETED
documentation: Engine is soft-deleted.

Detailed engine status.

See: https://api.dev.firebolt.io/devDocs#operation/coreV1GetEngine

classes.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_UNSPECIFIED
documentation: Status unspecified

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_STOPPED
documentation: Fully stopped.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_STARTING
documentation:
Provisioning process is in progress;
creating cloud infra for this engine.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_STARTING_INITIALIZING
documentation:
Provisioning process is complete;
waiting for PackDB cluster to initialize and start.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_RUNNING
documentation:
Fully started;
engine is ready to serve requests.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_UPGRADING
documentation:
Version of the PackDB is changing.
This is zero downtime operation that does not affect engine work.
This status is reserved for future use (not used fow now).

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_RESTARTING
documentation:
Hard restart (full stop/start cycle) is in progress.
Underlying infrastructure is being recreated.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_RESTARTING_INITIALIZING
documentation:
Hard restart (full stop/start cycle) is in progress.
Underlying infrastructure is ready. Waiting for
PackDB cluster to initialize and start.
This status is logically the same as ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_STARTING_INITIALIZING,
but used during restart cycle.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_REPAIRING
documentation:
Underlying infrastructure has issues and is being repaired.
Engine is still running, but it's not fully healthy and some queries may fail.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_STOPPING
documentation: Stop is in progress.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_DELETING
documentation:
Termination is in progress.
All infrastructure that belongs to this engine will be completely destroyed.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_DELETED
documentation: Infrastructure is terminated, engine data is deleted.

class_variables.name: firebolt.service.V1.types.EngineStatusSummary.ENGINE_STATUS_SUMMARY_FAILED
documentation:
Failed to start or stop.
This status only indicates that there were issues during provisioning operations.
If engine enters this status,
all infrastructure should be stopped/terminated already.

Engine summary status.

See: https://api.dev.firebolt.io/devDocs#operation/coreV1GetEngine

        the desired method.

methods.name: firebolt.model.V1.FireboltBaseModel.jsonable_dict
documentation:
Generate a dictionary representation of the service that contains serialized
primitive types, and is therefore JSON-ready.

This could be replaced with something native once this issue is resolved:
https://github.com/samuelcolvin/pydantic/issues/1409

This function is intended to improve the compatibility with HTTPX, which
expects to take in a dictionary of primitives as input to the JSON parameter
of its request function. See: https://www.python-httpx.org/api/#helper-functions

documentation: Get an up-to-date instance of the engine from Firebolt.

methods.name: firebolt.model.V1.engine.Engine.start
documentation:
Start an engine. If it's already started, do nothing.

Args:
    wait_for_startup:
        If True, wait for startup to complete.
        If False, return immediately after requesting startup.
    wait_timeout_seconds:
        Number of seconds to wait for startup to complete
        before raising a TimeoutError
    verbose:
        If True, print dots periodically while waiting for engine start.
        If False, do not print any dots.

Returns:
    The updated engine from Firebolt.

methods.name: firebolt.model.V1.engine.Engine.stop
documentation: Stop an Engine running on Firebolt.

A Firebolt engine. Responsible for performing work (queries, ingestion).

Engines are configured in {py:class}`Settings
<firebolt.model.engine.EngineSettings>`
and in {py:class}`EngineRevisionSpecification
<firebolt.model.engine_revision.EngineRevisionSpecification>`.

functions.name: firebolt.db.connection.connect_v2
documentation:
Connect to Firebolt.

Args:
    `auth` (Auth) Authentication object
    `database` (str): Name of the database to connect
    `engine_name` (Optional[str]): Name of the engine to connect to
    `account_name` (Optional[str]): For customers with multiple accounts;
                                    if none, default is used
    `api_endpoint` (str): Firebolt API endpoint. Used for authentication
    `additional_parameters` (Optional[Dict]): Dictionary of less widely-used
                            arguments for connection

classes.name: firebolt.db.connection.Connection

methods.name: firebolt.db.connection.Connection.__enter__
documentation: Context manager support

Firebolt database connection class. Implements PEP-249.

Args:

    engine_url: Firebolt database engine REST API url
    database: Firebolt database name
    username: Firebolt account username
    password: Firebolt account password
    api_endpoint: Optional. Firebolt API endpoint. Used for authentication.

Note:
    Firebolt currenly doesn't support transactions so commit and rollback methods
    are not implemented.

classes.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor._raise_if_error
documentation: Raise a proper error if any

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor._validate_set_parameter
documentation: Validate parameter by executing simple query with it.

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor.execute
documentation:
Prepare and execute a database query.

Supported features:
    Parameterized queries: placeholder characters ('?') are substituted
        with values provided in `parameters`. Values are formatted to
        be properly recognized by database and to exclude SQL injection.
    Multi-statement queries: multiple statements, provided in a single query
        and separated by semicolon, are executed separatelly and sequentially.
        To switch to next statement result, `nextset` method should be used.
    SET statements: to provide additional query execution parameters, execute
        `SET param=value` statement before it. All parameters are stored in
        cursor object until it's closed. They can also be removed with
        `flush_parameters` method call.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query to execute
    parameters (Optional[Sequence[ParameterType]]): A sequence of substitution
        parameters. Used to replace '?' placeholders inside a query with
        actual values
    skip_parsing (bool): Flag to disable query parsing. This will
        disable parameterized, multi-statement and SET queries,
        while improving performance

Returns:
    int: Query row count.

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor.executemany
documentation:
Prepare and execute a database query.

Supports providing multiple substitution parameter sets, executing them
as multiple statements sequentially.

Supported features:
    Parameterized queries: Placeholder characters ('?') are substituted
        with values provided in `parameters`. Values are formatted to
        be properly recognized by database and to exclude SQL injection.
    Multi-statement queries: Multiple statements, provided in a single query
        and separated by semicolon, are executed separately and sequentially.
        To switch to next statement result, use `nextset` method.
    SET statements: To provide additional query execution parameters, execute
        `SET param=value` statement before it. All parameters are stored in
        cursor object until it's closed. They can also be removed with
        `flush_parameters` method call.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query to execute.
    parameters_seq (Sequence[Sequence[ParameterType]]): A sequence of
       substitution parameter sets. Used to replace '?' placeholders inside a
       query with actual values from each set in a sequence. Resulting queries
       for each subset are executed sequentially.

Returns:
    int: Query row count.

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor.is_db_available
documentation: Verify that the database exists.

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor.is_engine_running
documentation: Verify that the engine is running.

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.Cursor.__iter__
documentation: Iteration support

Class, responsible for executing queries to Firebolt Database.
Should not be created directly,
use {py:func}`connection.cursor <firebolt.async_db.connection.Connection>`

Args:

: description: Information about a single result row
  rowcount: The number of rows produced by last query
  closed: True if connection is closed, False otherwise
  arraysize: Read/Write, specifies the number of rows to fetch at a time

  > with the {py:func}`fetchmany` method

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.CursorV2._api_request
documentation:
Query API, return Response object.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query
    parameters (Optional[Sequence[ParameterType]]): A sequence of substitution
        parameters. Used to replace '?' placeholders inside a query with
        actual values. Note: In order to "output_format" dict value, it
            must be an empty string. If no value not specified,
            JSON_OUTPUT_FORMAT will be used.
    path (str): endpoint suffix, for example "cancel" or "status"
    use_set_parameters: Optional[bool]: Some queries will fail if additional
        set parameters are sent. Setting this to False will allow
        self._set_parameters to be ignored.

methods.name: firebolt.db.cursor.CursorV2.is_db_available
documentation:
Verify that the database exists.

Args:

Args:

    engine_url (str): URL of the engine

        set parameters are sent. Setting this to False will allow

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.cached_property
documentation:
cached_property implementation for 3.7 backward compatibility.

Args:
    func (Callable): Property getter

Returns:
    T: Property of type, returned by getter

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.prune_dict
documentation:
Prune items from dictionaries where value is None.

Args:
    d (dict): Dict to prune

Returns:
    dict: Pruned dict

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.mixin_for
documentation:
Define mixin with baseclass typehint.

Should be used as a mixin base class to fix typehints.

Args:
    baseclass (Type[TMix]): Class which mixin will be made for

Returns:
    Type[TMix]: Mixin type to inherit from

Examples:
    ```
    class ReadonlyMixin(mixin_for(BaseClass))):
        ...
    ```

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.fix_url_schema
documentation:
Add schema to URL if it's missing.

Args:
    url (str): URL to check

Returns:
    str: URL with schema present

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.get_auth_endpoint
documentation:
Create auth endpoint from api endpoint.

Args:
    api_endpoint (URL): provided API endpoint

Returns:
    URL: authentication endpoint

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.get_internal_error_code
documentation:
Get internal error code from response.

Args:
    response (Response): HTTP response

Returns:
    Optional[int]: Internal error code

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.merge_urls
documentation:
Merge a base and merge urls.

If merge is not a relative url, do nothing

Args:
    base (URL): Base URL to merge to
    merge (URL): URL to merge

Returns:
    URL: Resulting URL

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util._print_error_body
documentation: log error body if it exists, since it's not always logged by default

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.raise_errors_from_body
documentation:
Process error in response body. Only raise errors if the json body
can be parsed and contains errors. Otherwise, let the rest of the code
handle the error.

Args:
    resp (Response): HTTP response

functions.name: firebolt.utils.util.parse_url_and_params
documentation: Extract URL and query parameters separately from a URL.

functions.name: firebolt.utils.usage_tracker._os_compare
documentation:
System-independent path comparison.

Args:

    expected: expected file path

Returns:
    True if file ends with path

functions.name: firebolt.utils.usage_tracker.get_sdk_properties
documentation:
Detect Python, OS and SDK versions.

Returns:
    Python version, SDK version, OS name and "ciso" if imported

functions.name: firebolt.utils.usage_tracker.detect_connectors
documentation:
Detect which connectors are running the code by parsing the stack.
Exceptions are ignored since this is intended for logging only.

functions.name: firebolt.utils.usage_tracker.format_as_user_agent
documentation:
Return a representation of a stored tracking data as a user-agent header.

Args:
    connectors: Dictionary of connector to version mappings

Returns:
    String of the current detected connector stack.

functions.name: firebolt.utils.usage_tracker.get_user_agent_header
documentation:
Return a user agent header with connector stack and system information.

Args:
    user_drivers(Optional): User-supplied list of tuples of all drivers
        and their versions intended for tracking. Driver is a programmatic
        module that facilitates interaction between a clients and underlying
        database.
    user_clients(Optional): User-supplied list of tuples of all clients
        and their versions intended for tracking. Client is a user-facing
        module or application that allows interaction with the database
        via drivers or directly.

Returns:
    String representation of a user-agent tracking information

classes.name: firebolt.utils.usage_tracker.ConnectorVersions
documentation: Verify correct parameter types

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.FireboltError
documentation: Base class for all Firebolt errors.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.FireboltEngineError
documentation: Base class for engine related errors.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.EngineNotRunningError
documentation: Engine that's being queried is not running.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.EngineNotFoundError
documentation: Engine with provided name was not found.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.DatabaseNotFoundError
documentation: Database with provided name was not found.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.InstanceTypeNotFoundError
documentation: Instance type with provided name was not found.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.NoAttachedDatabaseError
documentation:
Engine that's being accessed is not running.

Args:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

Attributes:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.AlreadyBoundError
documentation: Engine is already bound to a database.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.FireboltDatabaseError
documentation: Base class for database related errors.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.AccountNotFoundError
documentation:
Account with provided name doesn't exist.

Args:
    account_name (str): Name of account that wasn't found

Attributes:
    account_name (str): Name of account that wasn't found

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.AccountNotFoundOrNoAccessError
documentation:
Account with provided name doesn't exist.

Args:
    account_name (str): Name of account that wasn't found

Attributes:
    account_name (str): Name of account that wasn't found

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.AttachedEngineInUseError
documentation:
Engine unavailable because it's starting/stopping.

Args:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

Attributes:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.ConnectionError
documentation: Base class for connection related errors.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.ConnectionClosedError
documentation: Connection operations are unavailable since it's closed.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.CursorError
documentation: Base class for cursor related errors.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.CursorClosedError
documentation:
Cursor operations are unavailable since it's closed.

Args:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

Attributes:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.QueryNotRunError
documentation:
Some cursor methods are unavailable before a query is run.

Args:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

Attributes:
    method_name (str): Method that caused the error

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.AuthenticationError
documentation:
Firebolt authentication error.

Stores error cause and authentication endpoint.

Args:
    api_endpoint (str): Environment api endpoint used for authentication
    cause (str): Reason for authentication failure

Attributes:
    api_endpoint (str): Environment api endpoint used for authentication
    cause (str): Reason for authentication failure

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.AuthorizationError
documentation:
Firebolt authentication error.

Args:
    cause (str): Reason for authorization failure

Attributes:
    cause (str): Reason for authorization failure

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.Warning
documentation:
Base class for warning exceptions.

Exception raised for important warnings, like data truncations while inserting, etc.
PEP-249

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.InterfaceError
documentation:
Database interface related error.

Exception raised for errors that are related to the database interface
rather than the database itself.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.DatabaseError
documentation: Exception raised for errors that are related to the database.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.DataError
documentation:
Data processing error.

Exception raised for errors that are due to problems with the processed data,
like division by zero, numeric value out of range, etc.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.OperationalError
documentation:
Database operating error.

Exception raised for errors that are related to the database's operation
and not necessarily under the control of the programmer, e.g., an unexpected
disconnect occurs, the data source name is not found, a transaction could not
be processed, a memory allocation error occurred during processing, etc.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.IntegrityError
documentation:
Database data integrity error.

Exception raised when the relational integrity of the database is affected,
e.g., a foreign key check fails.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.InternalError
documentation:
Database internal error.

Exception raised when the database encounters an internal error,
e.g., the cursor is not valid anymore, the transaction is out of sync, etc.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.ProgrammingError
documentation:
Database programming error.

Exception raised for programming errors, e.g., table not found or already exists,
syntax error in the SQL statement, wrong number of parameters specified, etc.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.NotSupportedError
documentation:
Operation not supported.

Exception raised in case a method or database API was used which is not supported
by the database, e.g., requesting a .rollback() on a connection that
does not support transaction or has transactions turned off.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.ConfigurationError
documentation: Invalid configuration error.

classes.name: firebolt.utils.exception.FireboltStructuredError
documentation: Base class for structured errors received in JSON body.
